steps.title: Identify what the hypothesis requires
steps.content: The hypothesis claims intermediate-headed fish will be more likely than narrow-headed fish to detect obstructions in low-visibility conditions with large vortices. Support requires direct evidence that intermediate-headed fish outperform narrow-headed fish.

steps.title: Match the direct comparison
steps.content: Choice A directly compares narrow-headed sturgeon to intermediate-headed smooth-hound and shows the narrow-headed fish bumped into obstructions more often—proving intermediate-headed fish detected them better. The other three either measure only one head type's performance without comparison or examine variation within a single species, so none establish the required relative advantage. That matches choice A — A is correct.
